ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — leavened flatbread (naan); butter-enriched variant mapped to closest FoodEx2 bread category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for butter naan specifically; mapped to generic leavened bread category A000F as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for naan bread; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard butter naan piece weighs approximately 90 g and is typically served as an accompaniment to Indian curries or dal.

nutrient_source.title: Naan (butter) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Foodo record for Naan (butter), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread (FDC ID 174924) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; adaptation documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 71 assigned based on white wheat bread / refined-flour flatbread analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database. Naan made from refined wheat flour (maida) is compositionally equivalent to high-GI white bread products. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (71 × 47.7 × 0.90) / 100 ≈ 30.6 for a 90 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 561 mg per 100 g — well above the 400 mg moderate-concern threshold and approaching the 600 mg hard-gate limit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit butter naan to half a piece per meal (≈45 g, ~253 mg sodium) and pair with potassium-rich accompaniments such as lentil dal or spinach to partially offset the sodium load; requesting plain naan without butter brushing further reduces sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~71) from refined wheat flour (maida) combined with 47.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g produces a glycemic load of ~30.6 per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict butter naan to one small piece (≈45 g) per meal, pair it with high-protein or high-fibre accompaniments such as dal or raita to blunt the postprandial glucose spike, and consider wholemeal or multigrain flatbread alternatives with a lower glycemic index.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~5.8 g per 100 g from butter exceeds the 5 g threshold; trans fat ~0.1 g per 100 g from dairy butter is additionally pro-atherogenic; sodium 561 mg per 100 g raises blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit butter naan to occasional small portions, request naan prepared with olive oil or a minimal amount of unsaturated fat instead of butter, and account for the combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium burden when planning daily dietary targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~5.8 g per 100 g from butter exceeds the 5 g RA threshold; refined wheat flour drives high glycaemic load promoting systemic inflammation; trace trans fat (~0.1 g per 100 g) is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of butter naan and instead choose wholemeal or multigrain flatbreads prepared with anti-inflammatory fats such as olive oil to reduce saturated fat, trans fat, and glycaemic load burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter naan is a leavened Indian flatbread made from refined wheat flour (maida), plain yoghurt, eggs, and ghee or butter, traditionally cooked in a tandoor oven but commonly pan-fried in Singapore and other diaspora contexts. The bread is soft, slightly chewy, and mildly tangy from the yoghurt, with a rich buttery flavour from the butter or ghee brushed on the surface after cooking. It is a staple accompaniment to Indian curries, dal, and grilled meats across South Asian cuisine and is widely available in Indian restaurants throughout Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, butter naan is energy-dense at 325 kcal per 100 g, with 47.7 g carbohydrate predominantly from refined starch, 11.13 g fat (of which approximately 5.8 g is saturated fat from butter), 8.5 g protein, and a notably high sodium content of 561 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 71 (high), making it a high-glycaemic-load food unsuitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes without careful portion control. The high sodium content raises concern for hypertension management, and the saturated fat level exceeds thresholds relevant to cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Dietary fibre is moderate at 3.84 g per 100 g. Clinically, butter naan is best consumed in small portions paired with high-fibre or high-protein accompaniments, and wholemeal or multigrain flatbread alternatives are recommended for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a butter naan?
What is the GI of naan?
Is butter naan suitable for hypertension?
How much carbohydrate is in naan?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database with confidence 0.95 for an exact record match. Saturated fat, micronutrients, and minor fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread (FDC ID 174924) as the closest approved secondary source; adaptation documented. GI assigned from University of Sydney GI Database analogous refined-flour flatbread evidence.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (3.1 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.2 g/100 g), trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol_mg (45 mg/100 g), potassium_mg (130 mg/100 g), calcium_mg (72 mg/100 g), iron_mg (2.1 mg/100 g), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.18 mg/100 g), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.12 mg/100 g), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (1.8 mg/100 g), phosphorus_mg (110 mg/100 g), magnesium_mg (22 mg/100 g), zinc_mg (0.8 mg/100 g), and water_g (29.5 g/100 g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, Naan bread (FDC ID 174924), as HPB Foodo does not report these fields for this record. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available in approved sources): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg — detailed micronutrient data for butter-enriched naan not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy naan entry as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour flatbread equivalents from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for butter naan was found in the approved GI source. 4. FoodEx2 code A000F is the closest defensible bread category anchor; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for butter naan. The match_type has been corrected to 'closest_parent' from 'analogous_estimate' to align with FoodEx2 ontology guidelines. 5. The input dietary_tag 'high_fibre' has been removed as 3.84 g/100 g dietary fibre does not meet the Singapore HPB high-fibre threshold of ≥6 g/100 g for solids. 6. Trans fat of 0.1 g/100 g is a trace estimate from dairy butter content; this is pro-inflammatory and clinically relevant for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease.
